accept: A fair exchange. The {faction} accepts your offer.
Your terms are reasonable. We will proceed with the trade.
This bargain benefits both sides. Accepted.
reject: Your offer is unacceptable. The {faction} rejects this bargain.
You undervalue what we provide. Return with a serious offer.
No. This trade weakens us more than it helps us.
counteroffer: Your offer is close, but not enough. We propose: {counteroffer}.
The deal has potential. Adjust your terms to {counteroffer}, and we may agree.
We will not accept as written. Our counteroffer is {counteroffer}.
taboo: We will not trade in {resource}. Offer something else.
That resource is forbidden among our people. Remove it from the offer.
shortage: We cannot provide resources we do not hold.
Our stores do not contain enough to satisfy that request.
